title: 10ft Refrigerated Container / Freezer
shortDescription: A 10ft refrigerated container (freezer) with roughly 13 m³ of thermally regulated space, cooling to -40°C, weighing approximately 2,870 lbs.
description:
The smallest reefer in our lineup, this 10ft refrigerated container (also used as a compact walk-in freezer) offers roughly 13 m³ of thermally regulated space — a practical size for event cooling, tight jobsites, and construction sites where a full 20ft or 40ft reefer would be overkill. It holds a working range of -25°C to 25°C with about one degree of accuracy, and humidity is separately adjustable between 50% and 99% for goods that need more than just temperature control. The unit runs on a 5-pole 32A power supply, with single- and three-phase options available depending on your site.

Construction is 14-gauge steel with reinforced corner posts, a stainless steel and aluminum interior that's easy to keep clean, and a flat or T-grid floor. Fully lockable double doors are standard, with a butcher-door configuration available and an internal emergency release so no one can get trapped inside — doors ship uninstalled and are fitted on arrival. You can also choose standard barn doors or a lighter 6-foot roll-up door if you don't want to deal with the heavier barn-door swing. The unit weighs approximately 2,870 lbs and carries a valid CSC plate for freight use.

One sizing note: ocean freight has a 20ft minimum, so this 10ft container can't ship alone by sea — only paired with another 10ft unit or as mixed cargo. Available in new and used condition.

title: Boxabl Casita homes
shortDescription: The Boxabl Casita is a factory-built, foldable tiny home, shipped compact and unfolded on-site for fast installation.
description:
This is a Boxabl Casita — a factory-built, foldable home designed to fold down to roughly 8 feet wide for standard highway transport, avoiding the wide-load escorts and signage a typical 14-foot-wide modular home requires. It ships in its folded state, remains upright throughout transport, and is delivered by crane and semi-truck, unfolding on site in about a day. The kitchen and bathroom are built into one side of the unit and stay intact through shipping, and the unit bolts to a foundation using connector plates once positioned.

The bathroom includes a large counter, backlit mirror, vessel sink, and a deep shower or tub behind a sliding glass barn door. Delivery includes the fully finished interior, cabinets and windows included, but you'll need to prepare the foundation and arrange utility hookups in advance unless the unit will run off-grid.

The Casita works as a standalone small home or as an accessory dwelling unit (ADU) — a secondary structure on a lot with an existing home, useful for rental income or housing family, and permitted in many areas where a tiny house on wheels is not.

title: 18,000 Gallon ASME Storage Tank on Skids
shortDescription: An 18,000-gallon ASME skid-mounted storage tank rated to 250 PSI, engineered for practical, reliable bulk LPG storage, in new and used condition.
description:
This 18,000-gallon ASME storage tank comes mounted on skids, making it a practical option for sites that need propane, NGL, or butane storage without pouring a foundation or running underground piping — it's built for minimal setup and can be relocated or reconfigured to a new site as needed.

The vessel is newly manufactured, ASME Section VIII Division 1 code compliant, and National Board certified. Unlike a bare pressure vessel, it ships fully valved at the factory and leak tested, built to an oilfield-rugged construction standard. Internal valves include thermal-kill, pneumatic-kill, and excess-flow-kill protection, backed by a remote pneumatic kill switch mounted on a stand with 30 feet of hose, plus brackets for a nitrogen bottle and a customer air-supply quick connect. Landing gear stabilizing struts add stability, and the unit is fully wired and plumbed with standard tractor connections, complete with the safety decals and placards required to meet DOT and NFPA standards. A pintle anchor and container locks provide redundant attachment for the removable axle assembly, and a junction box is mounted and wired for a high-level switch.

Plumbing includes a 2-inch liquid-in line with a back-flow check valve, a 2-inch sight-flow back-check, and a 2-inch globe valve on the driver's side, while the curbside bulkhead carries a 2-inch liquid-out and 1¼-inch vapor connection with tripod pull-aways, plus angle valves housed in a lock box to guard against unauthorized unloading. A separate liquid and vapor outlet, each with valves and excess-flow protection, is provided for dewatering, and a combination valve lets you check tank level without a ladder. The low-voltage high-level switch can be wired normally open or normally closed at the frame-mounted junction box, and the tank volume gauge is built to hold its accuracy even under the shock loading of being transported empty. Standard finish is epoxy primer, with a urethane topcoat available as an option. Available in both New and Used condition.

Every propane tank is only ever filled to about 80 percent of its rated capacity, to leave room for the propane to expand, so the true usable volume runs below the tank's nominal size. title: Thermo King TriPac Evolution Diesel APU | Auxiliary Power Units
shortDescription: The Thermo King TriPac Evolution is a 3rd-generation diesel auxiliary power unit (APU) that can cut fuel consumption by up to 2,500 gallons per year.
description:
The Thermo King TriPac Evolution is a 3rd-generation diesel auxiliary power unit (APU) that lets a truck's sleeper cab run heating, air conditioning, and onboard electronics without idling the main engine. It is built around a 2-cylinder, Tier IV Final-compliant diesel engine driving a 13,000 BTU air conditioning system (compressor, evaporator, and controller — charged with R-134a at installation) and a 7,500 BTU fuel-fired heater, along with a single HVAC controller and a 65-amp alternator. The unit is DPF-compatible and available in two compliance configurations: a standard model that is 49-state EPA Tier 4 compliant for aftermarket installs, and a version with a passive aftertreatment device (ATD) that adds 50-state compliance for California Air Resources Board (CARB) requirements, using metal mesh technology for fast regeneration in as little as eight minutes.

By eliminating the need to idle the tractor engine for cab power, the TriPac Evolution cuts fuel use by roughly 75% compared to idling — on the order of 2,500 gallons a year in typical use — while reducing engine wear, maintenance costs, and emissions. It is designed for a 2,000-hour maintenance interval, extended further by an auto start/stop function that only runs the unit when needed. Controls use a three-knob, automotive-style layout with a color digital display, and the system includes a data logger with 22 alarm codes for diagnostics, flash-loadable firmware for both the controller and the HMI, and programmable temperature control. TracKing telematics comes standard with 24 months of complimentary service, covering APU run hours, cabin temperature, automated fuel tax reporting, real-time alarm notifications, maintenance reminders, and over-the-air software updates.

Optional accessories include an Air Tower that improves cab air circulation and can cut APU run time by up to 22%, and a ThermoLite solar panel that can reduce engine run time by up to 30%. The TriPac Evolution line carries a manufacturer warranty of four years on major components and two years bumper-to-bumper.

title: Twin 1000 Gallon Nh3 Tanks
shortDescription: A twin 1,000-gallon anhydrous ammonia (NH3) tank trailer rated to 265 PSI, mounted on 10-ton P&H gears with rectangular tube construction.
description:
This unit pairs two 1,000-gallon anhydrous ammonia (NH3) nurse tanks on a shared tandem-axle running gear, built by Arcosa. Each tank is constructed of carbon steel to ASME code and rated up to 250 psi at 125°F. As with all NH3 nurse tanks, every wetted component — valves, fittings, and the tank shell itself — is mild or stainless steel; NH3 service never uses galvanized metals, since ammonia reacts with zinc coatings. The tanks are painted white or another light color to reflect sunlight and limit heat buildup, which helps keep internal pressure in check.

Nurse tanks like these are used to move liquid anhydrous ammonia under pressure from a bulk storage facility out to field application equipment for agricultural use. Fill valves, the vapor return valve, the pressure gauge, and the liquid level gauges are all mounted at the top front of the tank, protected by a roll cage, and — per safety code — a tank is never filled beyond 85 percent of capacity to leave room for vapor expansion. A pressure relief valve is standard on each tank and is set to release vapor if internal pressure exceeds 250 psi, closing again once pressure drops back down.

Running gear, hitch style, and tire specification vary by unit — contact our team to confirm the exact configuration and condition of the trailer currently in stock.

title: Vector 8500 Trailer Refrigeration Units
shortDescription: The Carrier Vector 8500 is an all-electric trailer refrigeration system with ecoFORWARD technology, EPA Tier 4 compliant.
description:
The Carrier Transicold Vector 8500 is a hybrid diesel-electric trailer refrigeration unit built around Carrier's E-Drive all-electric technology: a diesel engine drives an onboard generator, and that generator powers a fully electric refrigeration and heating system rather than a conventional belt-driven compressor. Because the system has far fewer moving parts than a traditional mechanical unit — no alternator, drive belts, idlers, vibrasorbers, compressor shaft seals, or clutches — maintenance requirements are reduced and uptime improves. The unit's ecoFORWARD technologies deliver fuel savings and meet EPA Tier 4 emissions regulations, and components switch on only when needed rather than running continuously. A built-in electric standby feature lets the unit run on shore power while parked for loading or staging, cutting fuel use, noise, and emissions during that time. The Vector 8500 is available in single- and multi-temperature configurations and runs on the APX control system.

At 100°F ambient on high-speed diesel or electric standby operation, cooling capacity is 60,000 BTU/hr (17,585 W) at a 35°F setpoint, 35,000 BTU/hr (10,260 W) at 0°F, and 24,000 BTU/hr (7,035 W) at -20°F, with evaporator airflow of 3,100 cfm (5,270 m³/hr). The unit weighs approximately 1,810 lbs (820 kg) including electric standby; condenser dimensions are 76.4″ x 85.7″ x 22.8″, evaporator dimensions are 66.3″ x 45.2″ x 8.2″, and the body opening is 66.8″ x 46.0″. title: Vector 8611MT Units
shortDescription: The Carrier Vector 8611MT is an all-electric multi-temperature trailer unit with two evaporators built in for a center-divide configuration without a remote evaporator.
description:
The Carrier Transicold Vector 8611MT is a multi-temperature trailer refrigeration unit built on the all-electric E-Drive Vector platform, designed for trailers split lengthwise by a center dividing wall. Rather than requiring a separate remote evaporator for the second compartment, it incorporates two evaporators and fans directly into the main unit, creating two independently controlled refrigerated zones — useful for food service and grocery distribution operations hauling mixed perishable and frozen loads. Each zone has independent air control for optimized airflow and pulldown, and the unit uses damperless defrost with consistent all-electric heating rather than hot gas heat, which lets it shut down the compressor and other refrigeration components while heating for additional fuel savings.

A distinguishing feature of the 8611MT is its 15-inch side-to-side center-wall placement flexibility, versus competitive systems that require the trailer to be split exactly down the middle — this can accommodate up to 17% more cargo. The dual-evaporator design also eliminates most check and solenoid valves, meaning fewer braze joints and fewer potential refrigerant leak points than mechanically interconnected competitive units. Diesel power comes through a 21 kVA generator that runs the all-electric refrigeration system (E-Drive), with built-in electric standby so the unit can run at full refrigeration capacity from shore power while parked, without engine noise, emissions, or fuel use. Cooling capacity is 55,000 BTU/hr at a 35°F setpoint, with strong performance at freezing setpoints as well. For applications needing a third refrigerated compartment, the system is pre-configured for an optional remote evaporator from Carrier's line of full-width, half-width, single-, and dual-discharge smart evaporators, and the unit is compatible with Carrier's eSolutions telematics for remote monitoring.

description:

Carrier's EcoFORWARD technologies are built into the unit to raise cooling output while cutting fuel use: the X4 7500 achieves 35 to 50 percent more BTUs of cooling per gallon of fuel than the platform it replaces, along with roughly 3 to 10 percent higher cooling capacity, pulldown up to 20 percent faster, fuel consumption 5 to 22 percent lower (up to 35 percent lower during pulldown), 24 percent less refrigerant required, and reduced engine runtime — improvements driven by optimized V-Force fans, Novation micro-channel condenser coils, and a standard electronic expansion valve in place of the mechanical valves used on earlier models.

Power comes from a Tier 4 Final V2203L diesel engine — 4-cylinder, 2.2L direct injection, rated at 24.8 hp (18.5kW) at 1,800 rpm — paired with a CAN-bus electronic interface, a 70-amp alternator, and Carrier's Extended Service Interval (ESI) package, which stretches service to every 3,000 hours. title: X4 7700 Trailer Refrigeration Units
shortDescription: The Carrier X4 7700 is the newest member of the X4 family, delivering 20-25% fuel savings and lifetime CARB compliance in a single-temperature unit.
description:
The Carrier Transicold X4 7700 is the newest and most efficient member of the X4 family of single-temperature trailer refrigeration units, built for fleets that need both high capacity and long-term California compliance. It runs a three-speed Kubota V2403 smart diesel engine that adds a low-RPM “eco speed” to the standard high and low speeds, automatically engaging when ambient and internal temperatures allow for additional fuel savings; common-rail fuel injection improves fuel delivery efficiency by 5–10%. Paired with a proven 05G 41-CFM six-cylinder compressor, the unit delivers demonstrated fuel savings of 20–25% versus a standard X4 unit and a 96% reduction in particulate emissions.

Emissions hardware includes an enhanced fuel filtration system and a maintenance-free diesel oxidation catalyst, which push particulate, hydrocarbon, and NOx emissions down without the complex exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) that competing systems require. The engine is EPA Tier 4 compliant for engines under 25 hp and meets CARB's 2022 Airborne Toxic Control Measure amendments for transport refrigeration units, giving the X4 7700 lifetime CARB compliance in California without a diesel particulate filter or future exhaust system upgrades. Refrigerant is R-452A, which has roughly 45% lower global warming potential than the R-404A used in standard X4 units.

Every X4 7700 comes with Carrier's Lynx Fleet telematics hardware built in for remote monitoring of temperature, location, movement, and system performance, with a factory-installed TRU-Mount solar panel available as an option to help maintain battery charge for the unit and its telematics system. Like the rest of the X4 line, it combines high refrigeration capacity, fast pulldown, and precise temperature control with a straightforward, practical design intended to reduce engine run hours and maintenance costs.

title: Shipping Container 7' Wide Rollup Door Header Plate
shortDescription: A header plate sized specifically for a 7-foot wide roll-up door conversion, required when replacing standard cargo doors with a 7' roll-up door.
description: A header plate — sometimes called a top end rail — is required only when installing a 7-foot-wide roll-up door in place of a container's original cargo doors. It caps off the top of the roll-up door opening and protects the door's roll-up barrel from the weather, keeping rain and moisture from getting into the container. Installation is by welding the plate to the top and side of the container. If you're removing your cargo doors and fitting a 7-foot roll-up door, this plate is a required part of that conversion.

title: Shipping Container Door
shortDescription: A replacement shipping container door, available in 4 different sizes.
description:
This shipping container man door is part of a Weld-N-Go product line designed for straightforward field installation: a steel-framed door unit that's cut into the container wall and welded in place, rather than requiring a full custom fabrication. It's available in four different sizes to fit different container and opening dimensions.

Each man door ships complete with a door threshold plate, a door sweep, a door rain drip guard, a door gasket, a deadbolt, and a door lever. Installation involves cutting out the container wall to the unit's outer dimensions — a cutting wheel is the recommended tool — and then welding the steel frame into place. The door can be positioned wherever it's needed on the container, and detailed installation instructions ship with every order.

title: Shipping Container Lock Box
shortDescription: A bolt-on shipping container lock box that fits standard 20' and 40' containers without welding, more secure than a standard padlock alone.
description:
This shipping container lock box is a heavy-gauge steel enclosure that bolts onto a container's cargo doors to protect the padlock shackle from bolt cutters, angle grinders, and other cutting tools. A padlock used alone leaves its shackle exposed and can be cut through in under ten seconds; enclosing it inside this steel housing, with a recessed bolt design that also resists prying, closes that gap.

Installation requires no welding. The kit includes a two-piece enclosure (left and right door halves), Grade 5 carriage bolts with hidden heads, backing plates that double as drill templates, washers, and mounting hardware, along with installation instructions. With a drill, a 5/8-inch bit, a 3/4-inch socket wrench, and basic measuring tools, installation typically takes 15 to 20 minutes: position the lock box at the preferred height (42–60 inches from the ground), mark the drill holes using the backing plate as a template, drill through the container doors, insert the carriage bolts through the lock box, door, and backing plate, then tighten. The finish is powder-coated steel, built to resist rust and hold up in outdoor conditions.

It fits standard ISO 20-foot and 40-foot shipping containers with traditional swing doors and locking-rod handles, across New (1-Trip), Multi-Trip, Wind and Water Tight, Cargo Worthy, and most As-Is container grades, though the hardware isn't designed for roll-up door conversions, damaged or modified locking rods, or other non-standard door configurations — contact us to confirm fit if your container has been modified. A padlock is not included; pair it with a heavy-duty disc lock or shrouded-shackle padlock rated for outdoor use. If your lock box arrives with a defect in materials or workmanship, we'll arrange a replacement or refund.

title: Shipping Container Office Kit
shortDescription: A container office conversion kit sized for a 20ft shipping container, bundling the components needed to outfit a container as a working office space.
description:
This conversion kit turns a shipping container into an insulated office space. It includes an insulated office package with R-13 insulation, two 36-inch by 36-inch dual-pane vinyl windows, and a 3068 man door — a heavy-duty, 3-hour fire-rated insulated door with non-removable hinge pins, shipped with all the hardware and accessories needed for that door. Climate control comes from an 8,000 BTU air conditioner with heat, protected by a dedicated A/C security cage.

The electrical package includes two 4-foot LED light fixtures, three plugs, one switch, a 125-amp breaker box with breakers, and an exterior junction box. Detailed installation instructions ship with the kit. The shipping container itself is not included — this kit supplies the components needed to convert a container you already own into a finished office.

title: Shipping Container Roll Up Door
shortDescription: A roll-up door for shipping container conversions, replacing standard cargo doors for faster, wider access.
description: This is a steel roll-up door kit built specifically for standard and high-cube shipping containers, and it ships with all the hardware and materials needed to assemble and install it. A handful of add-ons complete the installation depending on your container's configuration: a framing kit sized for the sidewall, nose, and tail of the container; threshold plates, which direct water away from the entrance to keep it from pooling at the door; and, if you're replacing the original cargo doors with a 7-foot-wide roll-up door specifically, a header plate to cap the top of the opening and keep weather out. Other door sizes and colors are available on request.

title: Shipping Container Roll Up Door Framing Kit
shortDescription: A framing kit for shipping container roll-up door installations, providing the structural frame the roll-up door mounts into.
description: This framing kit is built specifically for the sidewall, nose, and tail of a shipping container and completes the structural installation of a roll-up door. It's a necessary companion piece for anyone installing or already running a shipping container roll-up door.

title: Shipping Container Roll Up Door Threshold Plate
shortDescription: A threshold plate for shipping container roll-up door installations, sealing and protecting the floor-level transition at the door opening.
description: A threshold plate — sometimes called a crash plate — protects the entrance area of a shipping container by directing water away from the doorway rather than letting it pool at the sill. It's installed forward of the door sill and is a companion piece for anyone installing or already running a shipping container roll-up door.

title: Shipping Container Vents
shortDescription: Shipping container louvered vents with an 8x8 perma-coated insect screen and 21 air-foil curved vanes, tested to withstand 147 mph winds.
description:
We offer two vent styles for shipping containers. The louver vent is a heavy-duty, flush-mounted vent with an 8x8 perma-coated screen to keep insects out, machine tab-fold construction, and built-in weather protection to keep the elements from getting in.

The turbine vent is an all-aluminum, rust-free unit that uses wind to pull air through the container passively. It runs on permanently lubricated upper and lower ball bearings for long service life without maintenance, and its 21 air-foil curved vanes have rolled edges that deflect water rather than letting it drip through. A large flashing simplifies installation, and the vent is tested to withstand winds up to 147 mph. By venting hot air out of the container passively, it can help reduce the energy load on any cooling equipment inside.

title: Shipping Container Window Shutter
shortDescription: A shutter accessory sized to match our standard container window, adding a closeable cover over the window opening.
description: This shutter mounts over a shipping container window opening to give you control over privacy, light, and security. Close it when you want to block outside light or visibility, or simply want an added layer of protection over the glass. It is designed as a companion piece for anyone who has already installed one of our shipping container window kits.

title: Shipping Container Windows
shortDescription: Shipping container windows with a 2" x 4" square tube frame, security bars, and dual-pane glass, ready to install in about 1 hour.
description: Each window kit uses a Weld-N-Go system built specifically for shipping and storage container modification, so the frame welds directly onto the container wall rather than requiring a separate cutout jig. The kit includes a 2-inch x 4-inch square tube steel frame, integrated security bars for protection against forced entry, and dual-pane glass for better insulation than a single pane would offer. Installation takes about an hour once the window opening is cut, which makes it a straightforward add for anyone converting a container into an office, workshop, or living space. Matching shutters are available separately if you want the option to close off the window entirely.
